# [Antagonist Name]

Antagonists are characters — they live in this separate folder only because their files are spoiler-dense forward planning, not because they're a different kind of entity.

## Role
[Primary antagonist, Book N: [Title]. One line on what *kind* of antagonist — a rival, a systemic operator, a true believer, a cornered opportunist.]

## Overview
[Two or three paragraphs. Who they are, what they want, and the worldview that makes their actions feel justified *to them*. The strongest antagonists are never wrong about everything.]

## Background
[Where they came from and how they became this. The origin should make their present logic legible — you should be able to finish the sentence "she's never technically lying, she's just…"]

## Personality
[How they present, how they operate under pressure, what they will and won't do. Include their self-justification — the story they tell themselves.]

## The Scheme
[What they're actually doing in their book: mechanism, beneficiaries, victims. What trail does it leave? Which of the protagonists' skills is the natural counter to it?]

## Connection to the Cast
[How they intersect the main characters. The best antagonists press on a specific character's contradiction or secret.]

## After Their Book
[Are they defeated, stalled, converted, at large? Cross-reference `continuity.md`. An antagonist who survives is a series asset.]
